栽.origin: 木 (tree) with 𢦏 for the sound; it means to plant or grow.

sentence.en: We plant trees on the mountain.

桂.origin: 木 (tree) with 圭 for the sound; it names the fragrant osmanthus tree.

sentence.en: Osmanthus flowers are very fragrant.

桃.origin: 木 (tree) with 兆 for the sound; it names the peach tree.

sentence.en: This peach is very sweet.

框.origin: 木 (wood) with 匡 for the sound; it means a frame.

sentence.en: The photo is put into the frame.

案.origin: 安 for the sound over 木 (wood); it means a table, file, or case.

sentence.en: The answer to this question is correct.

桑.origin: A tree 木 with leaves on top; it names the mulberry tree.

sentence.en: Silkworms eat mulberry leaves.

档.origin: 木 (wood) with 当 for the sound; it means a shelf, file, or grade.

sentence.en: The teacher puts away the files.

桨.origin: Sounds like 将 on top, wood 木 below; it means an oar or paddle.

sentence.en: He rows hard with the oar.

桩.origin: 木 (wood) with 庄 for the sound; it means a stake or post.

sentence.en: There is a wooden stake on the ground.

桶.origin: 木 (once wooden) with 甬 for the sound; it means a bucket or barrel.

sentence.en: He carries a water bucket.

梁.origin: Water 氵 and wood 木: a wooden beam or bridge over water.

sentence.en: There is a bridge over the river.

梅.origin: 木 (tree) with 每 for the sound; it names the plum tree.

sentence.en: In winter the plum blossoms open.

梗.origin: 木 (tree) with 更 for the sound; it means a stem or stalk.

sentence.en: This flower's stem is very long.

梨.origin: 利 for the sound over 木 (tree); it names the pear tree.

sentence.en: Pears are sweet and crisp.

梳.origin: 木 (once wooden) with 㐬 for the sound; it means a comb.

sentence.en: She combs her hair with a comb.

检.origin: 木 part with 佥 for the sound; it means to check or inspect.

sentence.en: The doctor checks my body.

棉.origin: 木 (plant) and 帛 (cloth); the cotton plant that gives soft fiber.

sentence.en: Cotton is white and soft.

棋.origin: 木 (once wooden pieces) with 其 for the sound; it means chess.

sentence.en: Grandpa likes to play chess.

棍.origin: 木 (wood) with 昆 for the sound; it means a stick or rod.

sentence.en: The puppy holds a stick in its mouth.

棒.origin: 木 (wood) with 奉 for the sound; it means a club; also means great.

sentence.en: You did really great.

棚.origin: 木 (wood) with 朋 for the sound; it means a shed or shack.

sentence.en: There is a shed in the yard.

棱.origin: 木 (wood) with 夌 for the sound; it means an edge or ridge.

sentence.en: The stone's edges are very sharp.

楷.origin: 木 part with 皆 (all); the clear standard style of writing.

sentence.en: He writes beautiful regular script.

概.origin: 木 part with 既 for the sound; it means general or approximate.

sentence.en: He is probably eight years old.

榆.origin: 木 (tree) with 俞 for the sound; it names the elm tree.

sentence.en: There is an elm tree at the gate.

榜.origin: A notice 旁 posted on a board 木; it means a list or placard.

sentence.en: My older brother is my role model.

榨.origin: 木 (wood) with 窄 for the sound; it means to press out juice.

sentence.en: Mom squeezes juice for me.

槌.origin: 木 (wood) with 追 for the sound; it means a mallet or hammer.

sentence.en: He picks up the drumstick and beats the drum.

槐.origin: 木 (tree) with 鬼 for the sound; it names the locust tree.

sentence.en: The locust tree blooms with white flowers.

槽.origin: 木 (wood) with 曹 for the sound; it means a trough or groove.

sentence.en: The bowls are in the sink.

樊.origin: 棥 (fence of wood) with 大 (big); it means a railing or enclosure; also a surname.

sentence.en: His surname is Fan.

模.origin: 木 part with 莫 for the sound; it means a model or pattern.

sentence.en: He made an airplane model.

横.origin: 木 part with 黄 for the sound; it means horizontal or across.

sentence.en: Please draw a horizontal line.

橘.origin: 木 (tree) with 矞 for the sound; it names the orange or tangerine.

sentence.en: Oranges are orange-colored.

橙.origin: 木 (tree) with 登 for the sound; it means orange, the fruit and color.

sentence.en: I want to eat an orange.

橱.origin: A wooden 木 cabinet 厨 (also the sound); it means a cupboard.

sentence.en: The bowls are kept in the cupboard.

橹.origin: 木 (wood) with 鲁 for the sound; it means a long oar or scull.

sentence.en: The fisherman rows the scull.

檐.origin: 木 (wood) with 詹 for the sound; it means the eaves of a roof.

sentence.en: The bird nests under the eaves.

欠.origin: A person with an open mouth yawning; it means to lack or to owe.

sentence.en: I owe him one yuan.

欣.origin: 斤 for the sound with 欠; it means happy or glad.

sentence.en: We admire the scenery together.

欧.origin: 区 for the sound with 欠; it stands for Europe.

sentence.en: He has been to Europe.

欲.origin: 谷 for the sound with 欠 (to lack); it means desire or to want.

sentence.en: I have a good appetite today.

歪.origin: 不 (not) over 正 (straight): not straight, so it means crooked.

sentence.en: This picture is hung crooked.

歼.origin: 歹 (death) with 千 (thousand, also the sound); to wipe out.

sentence.en: The army wiped out the enemy.

殊.origin: 歹 part with 朱 for the sound; it means special or different.

sentence.en: Today is a special day.

残.origin: 歹 (death) with 戋 for the sound; it means broken or to harm.

sentence.en: We should help people with disabilities.

段.origin: A hand using a chisel to cut stone into pieces; it means a section.

sentence.en: Please read this passage.

殷.origin: Shows care and attention; it means abundant or earnest; also an old dynasty name.

sentence.en: The host is very attentive.

毁.origin: Hands using a tool to smash a clay pot; it means to destroy.

sentence.en: The strong wind destroyed the house.

毋.origin: A form related to 母 (mother) with a stroke through it; it means do not.

sentence.en: This matter needs no doubting.
